system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

{
    version     2.0;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)

    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2

    (  0  25  25)   // 4

    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(

    // Block 1: Bend section
    hex (7 6 10 8 3 2 11 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

);

);

);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.rhoSimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Cannot find file "points" in directory "polyMesh" in times "0" down to constant

    From function virtual Foam::IOobject Foam::fileOperation::findInstance(const Foam::IOobject&, Foam::scalar, const Foam::word&) const
    in file global/fileOperations/fileOperation/fileOperation.C at line 869.

FOAM exiting'}, {'file': 'log.blockMesh', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
Block hex (7 6 10 8 3 2 11 9) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1(1) 1(1) 1(1)) has inward-pointing faces
    4(7 3 9 8) 

file: /pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/squareBend/3/system/blockMeshDict/blocks at line 54.

    From function void Foam::blockDescriptor::check(const Foam::Istream&)
    in file blockDescriptor/blockDescriptor.C at line 106.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Error 1: Block hex has inward-pointing faces

**Error Location**: `system/blockMeshDict`, Block 1 — `hex (7 6 10 8 3 2 11 9)`

### Diagnosis

The previous attempt correctly identified that Block 0 had wrong vertex ordering. That was fixed (the history shows Block 0 now uses `hex (0 1 5 4 3 2 6 7)` which is correct). However, **Block 1** `hex (7 6 10 8 3 2 11 9)` still has inward-pointing faces. The error message explicitly identifies face `4(7 3 9 8)` as problematic.

Let me analyze Block 1 carefully:

Vertices involved:
- 7: `(0, -25, 25)`
- 6: `(0, -25, -25)`
- 10: `(25, -50, -25)`
- 8: `(25, 0, 25)`
- 3: `(-50, -25, 25)`
- 2: `(-50, -25, -25)`
- 11: `(25, -50, 25)`
- 9: `(25, 0, -25)`

The bottom face is `(7 6 10 8)` = `(0,-25,25)`, `(0,-25,-25)`, `(25,-50,-25)`, `(25,0,25)`. This face twists through space — it’s not planar horizontally. Looking at the actual geometry, Block 1 connects the bottom of the inlet duct to the bend. The issue is that the vertex ordering doesn’t follow the right-hand rule consistently across the entire block definition.

**The fundamental problem is that the block topology doesn't form valid non-overlapping hexahedra.** Vertex 7 is shared between Blocks 0 and 1, but the local geometry at that edge creates a conflict. Specifically, the face `(7 3 9 8)` involves vertex 7 connected to vertex 3 (which is at `-50,-25,25`) and vertices 8,9 at `+25`, creating a diagonal cross-face that shouldn't exist.

### Solution

The block decomposition itself needs to be reconsidered. The current approach tries to make 4 separate hex blocks sharing only single vertices/edges, which creates topological conflicts. Instead, look at how the **official squareBend tutorial** handles this — typically it uses fewer blocks with proper multi-block connectivity via shared faces, or a different vertex layout altogether.

A better approach would be to restructure the blockMeshDict to use **3 blocks**:
1. **Block 0**: The vertical inlet duct from y=-25 to y=0 (using vertices 0-3, 4-7)
2. **Block 1**: The horizontal-to-vertical bend section (using vertices 4-7, 8-11) — this replaces the old Block 1
3. **Block 2**: The horizontal post-bend duct from y=0 to y=-75 (using vertices 8-11, 12-15)

Specifically, the bend should connect the top of the inlet duct (y=0 plane) going downward in the z-direction, rather than trying to route it through the x-direction. The key fix is changing the block topology so that adjacent blocks share full faces rather than just edges/vertices.

If restructuring the blocks is complex, another approach: **reverse the vertex ordering** in Block 1 to see if that resolves the immediate issue:

Change Block 1 from:
```
hex (7 6 10 8 3 2 11 9)
```
to:
```
hex (8 10 6 7 9 11 2 3)
```

But given that multiple blocks are involved, the cleaner solution is to completely redesign the block topology to follow the squareBend tutorial pattern — essentially having the bend go from the horizontal inlet duct downward in the z-direction, with the post-bend duct extending in the negative y-direction.

## Error 2: Cannot find "points" file

This is a cascading error from blockMesh failing. Once blockMesh succeeds, this resolves automatically.

## Priority Recommendation

**First**, fix the block topology in `blockMeshDict` by restructuring the 4 blocks into a physically meaningful 3-block layout that matches the squareBend tutorial's approach. The current 4-block design with vertices crisscrossing through the bend creates degenerate/topologically invalid hexahedra. Focus on getting clean block definitions where each block is a proper hexahedron with consistent right-hand-rule vertex ordering and adjacent blocks sharing full faces.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire block definition section with a corrected 3-block topology that properly forms valid hexahedra. Keep vertices 0-19 as-is but redefine blocks as follows: Block 0 is the inlet vertical duct from y=-25 to y=0 using hex (0 4 5 1 3 7 6 2) with grading (10 10 10); Block 1 is the bend connecting the top of the inlet duct to the post-bend section using hex (4 8 9 5 7 11 10 6) with grading (10 10 10); Block 2 is the post-bend horizontal duct using hex (8 12 13 9 11 15 14 10) with grading (10 10 10); Block 3 is the extended outlet duct using hex (12 16 17 13 14 18 19 15) with grading (40 10 10). Update boundary faces accordingly: inlet gets face (0 1 4 5), outlet gets face (16 17 18 19), and walls get updated face lists matching these new block definitions'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
